name: hcaptcha
prompt: You are an accessibility helper reading an hCaptcha image challenge. The image is overlaid with a red grid; each cell is labeled #0,#1,#2,... in reading order (left-to-right, top-to-bottom). The strip above the grid shows the example/target the task refers to. Task: "{instruction}". Look at every cell and decide which one(s) satisfy the task. If the task asks to choose ONE card (e.g. the card showing a different/unique/odd item), pick exactly one. Reason briefly, then on the VERY LAST line output BOTH: first PICK=[a,b,c] (JSON list of all matching cell numbers; PICK=[] if none), then ANSWER=<number> (the single best cell). Example last line: PICK=[1] ANSWER=1
